text.title: Review Scenario Templates
text.summary: Four canonical review scenario templates: bug (wrong output), design (too many responsibilities), security (missing auth), performance (N+1 query) with concrete examples.

rule.statement: Classify each finding into exactly one of: bug, design, security, performance, or style. Run classification per finding, not per PR. Emit the matched scenario template format for each finding.
rule.rationale: Scenario classification forces consistent comment shape. Reviewers scanning comments can immediately identify the finding type and triage priority. Unconstrained comment formats produce inconsistent output that authors and reviewers interpret differently.

example.note: Discount calculation bug: percent treated as decimal produces wrong result.
Correct implementation with explicit contract in docstring.
example.code: def calculate_discount(price: float, discount_percent: float) -> float: """ Args: discount_percent: whole number (20 = 20%), not decimal (0.20). """ return price - (price * discount_percent / 100) # calculate_discount(100, 20) returns 80.0 (correct)

example.note: God method with too many responsibilities: validation, calculation, payment, email, inventory.
Single responsibility with dependency injection and async side effects via event bus.
Endpoint exposes any user's data without authentication or authorization.
Authentication required; authorization check prevents cross-user data access.
N+1 query: separate query for each order's items produces 102 queries for 100 orders.
Eager loading collapses N+1 into 1 query with JOINs.
